summary: Matched a large block of src/melee/lb/lbaudio_ax.c by implementing many previously stubbed audio AX helpers, adding static state/data definitions, correcting public prototypes, and adjusting callers in fighter, item, game-mode, menu sound test, un_2FC96, and baselib AXDriver. The decomp.dev bot reported +4760 matched bytes, +0.12% total matched code, and 36 new lbaudio_ax matches, with one small mnsoundtest improvement and one 1-byte mnsoundtest regression.
what_changed: Implemented many formerly unmatched lbaudio_ax functions, including lbAudioAx_80023694, lbAudioAx_800236DC, lbAudioAx_80023730, fn_80023750, lbAudioAx_800237A8, lbAudioAx_80023870, lbAudioAx_8002392C, fn_80023ED4, lbAudioAx_80023F28, lbAudioAx_80024030, lbAudioAx_800240B4, lbAudioAx_8002411C, lbAudioAx_80024184, lbAudioAx_80024304, lbAudioAx_8002438C, lbAudioAx_800245D4, lbAudioAx_800245F4, lbAudioAx_80024614, lbAudioAx_80024634, fn_80024654, lbAudioAx_80024B1C, lbAudioAx_80024B58, lbAudioAx_80024BD0, lbAudioAx_80024C08, lbAudioAx_80024C84, lbAudioAx_80024D50, lbAudioAx_80024D78, lbAudioAx_80024E50, lbAudioAx_80024E84, lbAudioAx_80024F08, lbAudioAx_8002500C, lbAudioAx_80025038, lbAudioAx_80025064, fn_800250A0, lbAudioAx_80028B2C, and lbAudioAx_80028B4C.
Added lbaudio data reconstruction, including the HPS filename pointer table lbl_803BC314 and small struct table lbl_803BC918, plus explicit data_pad arrays used to keep data layout aligned.
Split config/GALE01/symbols.txt data labels: str_testnz_hps became an 0xB string followed by lbl_803BC314, and str_lbAudioLoadData became a 0x10 string followed by lbl_803BC918.
Updated lbaudio_ax.h away from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for many APIs, adding concrete int, bool, void, const char*, and bool-argument prototypes.

Expanded lbaudio_ax.static.h with many static audio state variables around lbl_804D3874-lbl_804D38F4 and lbl_804D6408-lbl_804D6434, plus externs for lbl_804D63F0, lbl_804D63F4, lbl_804D63FC, lbl_804D6400, and lbl_804D6404.
Changed AXDriver_8038CFF4 signature to remove an unused float pitch parameter, and updated lbaudio wrapper fn_80023750 to call AXDriver_8038CFF4(sound_id, volume, pan, track, channel).
Corrected caller prototypes for audio volume/pan: ft_PlaySFX and Item_8026AE84/AF0C/AFA0 now take u8 volume/pan parameters at the public call boundary.
Changed gm_801601C4 and gm_80160244 from u8-returning functions to int-returning functions and updated local temporaries in gm_801602C0/gm_801603B0 accordingly.
Cleaned up mnsoundtest extern usage by including gm/gm_unsplit.h and gm/gmmain_lib.h, and passed gmMainLib_8015ED74() directly into gm_801601C4/gm_80160244 callsites.
Adjusted un_2FC96 audio setter callsites to load globals through temporary u8 variables before passing them to lbAudioAx_800245F4, lbAudioAx_80024614, lbAudioAx_800245D4, and lbAudioAx_80024634.
smart_moves: Centralized volume/pan normalization in fn_80023750: both volume and pan are doubled, clamped to 0..0xFF, then forwarded to AXDriver_8038CFF4. This allowed many public lbaudio wrappers to be small and matchable.

Used explicit int parameters for lbAudioAx_800237A8 and other lbaudio wrappers even when callers pass u8, while preserving u8 at external caller interfaces where needed; this avoided over-narrowing internal expressions.
Modeled invalid or sentinel sound IDs directly: lbAudioAx_800237A8 maps sfx_id >= 0x83D61 to a fallback call using 0x83D60, and lbAudioAx_80023870 keys off 0x83D61 to key off a specific track.
Replaced duplicated audio-stop reset code in lbAudioAx_80027DBC with a call to the newly matched lbAudioAx_800236DC, reducing duplicate code while matching the discovered helper.
Reconstructed data layout rather than leaving oversized generic symbols: HPS string/table boundaries in symbols.txt were split so C declarations could represent the true array layout.
Used small static inline helpers inside lbAudioAx_80023F28 to express filename lookup and stream-change logic without exposing extra symbols.
Preserved codegen-sensitive casts by introducing u8 temporaries in un_2FC96 before calling int-typed lbaudio setters.
Matched large volume-update logic in fn_80024654 by preserving repeated cached-value checks before calling HSD_SynthStreamSetVolume, HSD_SynthSFXUpdateAllVolume, and AXDriver_8038D914.
decomp_lessons: When an audio driver function appears to take u8 volume/pan in the low-level API, upstream wrappers may still need int arguments to match clamping, arithmetic, and ABI behavior.
Data symbol boundaries are often a blocker for large matches: splitting strings from following pointer/struct tables can unlock C definitions for the real data layout.
Do not trust early header guesses such as UNK_RET, UNK_PARAMS, or comments like 'i dont believe this actually returns anything'; matching can reveal concrete return values and callsite expectations.
For sound settings setters, clamping ranges are meaningful to matching: 0..0x7F for user-facing volume-like values and 0..0xFF after doubling for driver-facing volume/pan values.
Callsite-only changes can be necessary for a central file match: ft, item, gm, mn, un, and baselib prototype fixes all supported lbaudio_ax matching.
A small local type choice can matter: gm_801601C4/gm_80160244 returned int for matching even though values are capped at 0x7F.
Explicit padding arrays can be an acceptable tactic when reconstructing data regions before every object has a semantic name.
naming_conventions: Kept address-based function names for unknown lbaudio routines: lbAudioAx_80023F28, lbAudioAx_80024184, lbAudioAx_80024C84, etc.
Used fn_ prefix for unidentified local/helper functions such as fn_80023750, fn_80023ED4, fn_80024654, and fn_800250A0.
Used lbl_ address labels for unnamed data, including lbl_803BC314, lbl_803BC918, and many lbl_804D38xx/lbl_804D64xx static variables.
Used str_ labels for confirmed string objects in symbols.txt, including str_testnz_hps and str_lbAudioLoadData.

Preserved subsystem prefixes: lbAudioAx_ for library audio AX routines, AXDriver_ for baselib audio driver calls, ft_ for fighter, Item_ for item, gm_ for game-mode, and mnSoundTest_ for menu sound-test code.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Wrapper helper fn_80023750 clamps signed int volume/pan after multiplying by two, matching driver-facing u8 behavior without changing wrapper parameters to u8.
fn_80023ED4 similarly clamps stream volume to 0..0xFF and clamps a stream/index argument to 0..8 before AXDriver_8038E8EC.
Inserted explicit data_pad_0 and data_pad arrays around lbaudio data declarations to preserve object placement in the data section.
Used a concrete struct for lbl_803BC918 with three int fields, enabling table indexing in lbAudioAx_80024030.
Sound-track mapping in lbAudioAx_80024184 was expressed as a switch over sound IDs that rewrites the track argument before calling fn_80023750.
mnsoundtest gained direct nested calls like gm_801601C4(gmMainLib_8015ED74()), replacing separate unused-looking calls and helping the unmatched function improve by 9 bytes.
un_2FC96 introduced u8 tmp assignments before calls, a typical decomp tactic to force loads/truncations matching the original codegen.
The bot report confirms the tactics produced 36 new lbaudio_ax matches, with fn_80024654 alone contributing +1224 bytes.
review_feedback: No human review comments or PR body text were present in the provided slice.
Automated decomp.dev report for GALE01 showed matched code increased to 42.00% (+0.12%, +4760 bytes).
Automated report listed 36 new matches, mostly in main/melee/lb/lbaudio_ax, and one unmatched-function improvement in mnSoundTest_8024A790 from 86.46% to 88.39%.
Automated report also noted one small regression in mnsoundtest fn_8024AED0 from 94.72% to 94.62% (-1 byte).
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or type lbl_803BC314 HPS filename table entries.
Search for further matches of lbaudio_ax functions still left as /// # stubs after PR 1906, especially lbAudioAx_800233EC, lbAudioAx_80023B24, lbAudioAx_800243F4, fn_800244F4, and fn_800251EC.
Check whether the AXDriver_8038CFF4 removed float pitch parameter affected any other callsites or later prototype corrections.
Look for subsequent cleanup of lbl_804D38xx and lbl_804D64xx static audio state variable names.
Search for follow-up fixes to the reported -1 byte regression in mnsoundtest fn_8024AED0.
